decisions.title: Prioritize E2 UI stability vs new composable chart features
decisions.context: E2 UI locks (ENG-10024), UDF preview not updating (ENG-9721), and panel config issues signal core stability problems. Meanwhile, we're building composable chart infrastructure (ENG-9639, ENG-9030, ENG-5674). Need to decide: fix-first or parallel-track?

arguments.for: Nail the Core pillar demands stability. Broken UI erodes trust faster than new features add value. E2 is core product.
arguments.against: Composable charts enable Explore V2 and Canvas future. Parallel teams can address both. Customers requesting advanced viz.

decisions.title: Green-light CSRS production rollout or delay for caching validation
decisions.context: CSRS has SchemalessV2 reads (ENG-9986), caching logic (ENG-9457), and snapshot invalidation (ENG-9789) in flight. Catalog-optimizer failing on compacted assets (ENG-9958). This is critical for Build the Moat (query performance at scale).
decisions.blockedBy: ENG-9958 resolution and load testing results from ENG-9778

arguments.for: CSRS unlocks 10x query performance for large customers. Competitive moat. Early adopters ready to test.
arguments.against: Compaction issues could cause data corruption. Load testing incomplete. Premature launch risks customer trust.

decisions.title: Commit to MCP server strategy for Sift Edge architecture
decisions.context: Internal MCP server MVP (ENG-9296) suggests edge compute direction. Aligns with Sift Edge product roadmap and Embed Deeper pillar. Need architectural decision before Q2 planning.

arguments.for: MCP enables standardized edge-to-cloud integration. Customer demand for on-premise processing. Differentiation vs competitors.
arguments.against: Adds complexity to deployment model. Support burden. Unclear customer willingness to manage edge infrastructure.

decisions.title: Feature flag infrastructure: full rollout or extended beta?
decisions.context: Feature flag front-end prototypes done (ENG-9122), but ENG-9995 reveals failure modes need hardening. Critical for safe Canvas and Python Rules launches.

arguments.for: Need feature flags to derisk Canvas GA and Python Rules beta. Blocking multiple product launches. Team ready.
arguments.against: Current implementation returns errors on load failure. Could break production. Needs graceful degradation first (ENG-9995).

decisions.title: Accelerate Files in CEL for Canvas launch or descope
decisions.context: Files instrumentation (ENG-10014), YAML support (ENG-10013), and UI attachment (ENG-10015) in progress. Canvas depends on file handling for config-as-code workflows. ULA deploy (FD-318) may need this.

arguments.for: Files enable powerful Canvas use cases (importing configs, version control). Differentiator vs competitors. Customer requests.
arguments.against: Canvas can launch with manual file handling. Files add scope risk. Focus on core decision capture workflows first.

decisions.title: SCIM identity management workflow: build custom or adopt standard?
decisions.context: ENG-9925 defining workflow for email/identity changes on SCIM users. Embed Deeper pillar requires enterprise SSO maturity. Affects enterprise sales cycles.

arguments.for: Standard SCIM workflows reduce support burden. Enterprise buyers expect seamless IdP integration. Compliance requirement for some deals.
arguments.against: Custom workflow could offer better UX for edge cases. Standard may not cover all Sift-specific identity scenarios.

decisions.title: Multi-asset simulator: expand scope or maintain single-asset focus?
decisions.context: ENG-9856 building multi-asset support for simulator test runs. Enables fleet-level testing but adds complexity. Relates to Ask SIFT's ability to reason across assets.

arguments.for: Customers need fleet-level validation (e.g., satellite constellations, vehicle fleets). Unlocks new use cases. Competitive parity.
arguments.against: Single-asset testing covers 80% of use cases. Multi-asset adds state management complexity. Delays other simulator improvements.

timeAudit.weekSummary: 7 critical decisions pending, blocking Q2 roadmap lock. CSRS and feature flags on critical path for Canvas GA (May 1 target).
alerts: E2 UI stability issues escalating (3 customer complaints this week)
CSRS production decision blocks data platform OKRs
Feature flag failure mode (ENG-9995) could delay Canvas launch
Meeting load 80% of work week - recommend decision sprint to unblock
